mechanism_narrative: The Federal Funds Rate at 2.0% represents an accommodative stance relative to inflation (CPI at 219.181). As credit markets seize up during financial stress, the Fed's low rate floor becomes a binding constraint on further easing, forcing Treasury yields to rise as investors demand compensation for duration risk and credit uncertainty. The mechanism activates when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5% and UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.
regime_hint: financial crisis with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of instances when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 12-month windows during crisis periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 381,169 million dollars show contraction momentum as consumer confidence collapses during financial turmoil. Declining demand signals recession, which compresses long-term growth expectations and pushes 10-year Treasury yields downward as investors flee to safety and reduce inflation expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day forward periods despite RSAFS contraction, or correlation between RSAFS growth and yield direction is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.1% is rising sharply as financial crisis triggers mass layoffs. Employment deterioration (PAYEMS at 137,473 thousand) accelerates recession expectations, reducing long-term inflation and growth forecasts. This causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors reprice terminal rate expectations downward and demand safe-haven duration.
regime_hint: labor market shock during financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 60-day forward periods following UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and yield direction is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 110.3034 reflects supply-side stress as manufacturing output contracts during financial disruption. Falling supply-side capacity signals stagflation risk, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher as investors demand inflation risk premium despite recession concerns. This mechanism dominates when INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 5-day forward periods following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 6-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 965 thousand units represent a structural collapse in residential investment demand. This demand destruction signals severe recession, forcing 10-year Treasury yields downward as growth expectations collapse and the Fed is expected to cut rates aggressively. The mechanism is regime-conditional: it produces negative yield direction only when HOUST falls below 1,000 units AND UNRATE is rising.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1000 AND UNRATE rising > 0.2pp in prior month
regime_hint: housing-led recession with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 20-day forward periods when both HOUST < 1000 and UNRATE is rising, or Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 12-month windows in housing-crisis regimes.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 219.181 (index level) shows inflation remains elevated despite economic stress. When FEDFUNDS is constrained at 2.0% and inflation expectations remain sticky, the real interest rate becomes deeply negative. This negative real rate environment forces 10-year Treasury yields higher as investors demand compensation for real capital erosion and inflation risk premium.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day forward periods when real rates are deeply negative, or correlation between (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS) and yield direction is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11,740.3 billion (2017 dollars) is approaching a growth inflection point as financial crisis spreads. Declining GDP growth expectations compress the terminal growth rate used in discounted cash flow models, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward. This always-on mechanism reflects the structural relationship between long-term growth and long-duration asset pricing.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 60-day forward periods despite declining GDP growth expectations, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 8-quarter window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 6.1% combined with payroll employment at 137,473 thousand reveals a labor market in early-stage shock. When unemployment rises sharply (> 0.3pp monthly) while payrolls contract simultaneously, this signals a regime shift from cyclical slowdown to structural recession. The mechanism predicts 10-year Treasury yields fall as terminal rate expectations are revised downward and flight-to-safety demand increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp AND PAYEMS declines > 100 thousand in same month
regime_hint: structural labor market deterioration during financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of 20-day forward periods following simultaneous UNRATE spike and PAYEMS decline, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 6-month windows during labor shock episodes.
